name: camels-us

env_axis.name: country
env_axis.shift_mechanism: geographic-cohort within CONUS; paired with DK/IND for cross-country

ood_protocol.name: pub-cross-catchment
ood_protocol.ref: Newman et al. 2015 (dataset); Kratzert et al. 2019 HESS (PUB methodology)

facts: 671 watersheds × daily 1980-2014; EA-LSTM in-country median NSE≈0.74 (Kratzert 2019 HESS).
hypotheses: Country is a categorically different environment unit than held-out catchment, because held-out-catchment PUB shift does not share the structure of US→DK cross-country shift.
The 60 static attributes are insufficient to characterize the environment, because a hidden axis (e.g., climate zone) is confounded with country.
intuitions: US-internal PUB extensions of Kratzert 2019 (HESS) for 2024-25 likely exist.
CAMELS-US probably appears in causal-hydrology / Beven-school work that distinguishes process knowledge from data fit.
unknowns: 'Catchment' is an imposed cartographic partition, not a discovered one; there is no established framing for imposed-versus-discovered structural primitives, and this dataset surfaces that gap.
Whether daily resolution is the right time grain or coarsens away mechanism cannot be determined from the data.
epistemic_state.boundary: With C=0 and a causal-chain (not branching-mechanism) process, the dataset is silent on multi-pathway shift.

representational_commitments: Daily resolution coarsens sub-daily storm-hydrograph dynamics
60 static attributes are a curatorial selection
Three forcing datasets (Daymet, NLDAS, Maurer) — choice biases analysis
materialization_status: 671/671 gauges fetched via pip-installed camels package (Dhruv's pointer). 473 MB tarball: forcing + streamflow CSVs per gauge. ~12,784 daily rows per gauge × 671 ≈ 8.58 M total events.
